```python
# test_camel_to_snake.py
import pytest

from your_module import camel_to_snake  # change to the actual module name


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "inp, expected",
    [
        ("camelCase", "camel_case"),
        ("CamelCase", "camel_case"),
        ("simple", "simple"),  # no capitals
        ("already_snake", "already_snake"),  # underscore + no internal camel boundary
        ("HTTPRequest", "http_request"),
        ("JSONData", "json_data"),
        ("myURLValue", "my_url_value"),
        ("aB", "a_b"),
    ],
)
def test_camel_to_snake_happy_paths(inp, expected):
    assert camel_to_snake(inp) == expected


def test_camel_to_snake_edge_cases():
    # Empty string
    assert camel_to_snake("") == ""

    # Single character
    assert camel_to_snake("A") == "a"
    assert camel_to_snake("z") == "z"

    # Only uppercase letters (no [a-z][A-Z] boundary)
    assert camel_to_snake("ABC") == "abc"

    # Trailing/leading underscores remain unchanged other than lowercasing
    assert camel_to_snake("_LeadingUnderscore") == "_leading_underscore"
    assert camel_to_snake("TrailingUnderscore_") == "trailingunderscore_"

    # Consecutive capitals: boundary occurs before the last capital when preceded by lowercase
    # "TestHTTPServer" -> "test_http_server"
    assert camel_to_snake("TestHTTPServer") == "test_http_server"


@pytest.mark.parametrize("bad_value", [None, 123, 1.23, ["aB"], {"k": "v"}, object()])
def test_camel_to_snake_error_cases_type_errors(bad_value):
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        camel_to_snake(bad_value)
```

**Note:** Replace `from your_module import camel_to_snake` with the actual import path for your function.
